priceSnapshot.source: Fortune (Joseph Hostetler) — quoted market price at 5:45 a.m. ET
priceSnapshot.note: BTC is essentially flat overnight versus the ~$65,047 Jul 23 US close, defending the $65,000 handle at ~$65,030 (down ~0.95% versus the same time yesterday). Still inside the broader $64,000-$66,800 range for a third session; price is sitting inside the $64,800-$65,200 EMA-cluster support with the range floor / invalidation at $64,062 intact.

reduceIf.confirmation: 1H close below $64,062 loses the range floor / invalidation — scale the remaining core toward EXIT

addIf.confirmation: 4H close back above $65,500 reclaims the shelf lost on Jul 23 — re-add the tranche trimmed at midday

decisionBox.changed: The 7-session US spot BTC ETF inflow streak snapped overnight — Jul 23 printed a net OUTFLOW of ~$225M (IBIT ~-$202M), removing the bulls' main structural bid. BTC still held the $65,000 handle. Intel's post-close beat (rev +25% YoY) spiked its shares toward $110 then faded, softening but not reversing the risk-off tone GOOGL/TSLA set. Core stays HOLD above the $64,062 invalidation; the resting stop is the sole protection until midday.

atr.source: Not verified this run (exchange APIs blocked from sandbox). Prior verified daily ATR(14) ~$1,553 (~2.36%) on Jul 22 — deliberately NOT carried into any calculation here.

timeframes.structure: Chopping the $64,900-$65,200 pocket overnight with buyers defending the $65,000 handle; no impulsive break either way into the US morning.
timeframes.source: Price action per Fortune / Cryptonomist / CoinReporter (Jul 23-24); intraday indicator table not verified (exchange APIs blocked)

timeframes.structure: The $65,380/$65,500 shelf lost on Jul 23 still caps as resistance; holding the $65,000/$64,800 demand keeps this a range-low base rather than a fresh leg down.
Basing above the $64,062 range floor after the multi-session compression; reads as a range-low hold rather than a confirmed trend break while the ETF bid is absent.

The July repair (~13% off the month's low) is holding a higher-low structure and price sits above the ~$63,000 50-day SMA, but the daily EMA200 still sits above price so the higher-TF downtrend is intact. RSI not re-verified this run.
timeframes.source: Structure per Cryptonomist / CoinReporter (Jul 23-24); daily RSI not re-verified this run

scalpContext.note: Scalp only the edges — live signals come from the chart, not this report.

etfFlows.note: The 7-session US spot BTC ETF inflow streak (~$981M cumulative, Jul 14-23) SNAPPED on Jul 23 with a net OUTFLOW of ~$225.18M — IBIT led redemptions at ~$202M and MSBT was the lone meaningful inflow at ~$5.01M. The structural bid that had been the bulls' best card is, for now, gone; US spot funds remain roughly $4.84B net-negative on the year.

liquidations.note: CoinGlass shows BTC futures open interest ~$48.62B with ~$54.2M total liquidations over the trailing 24h; a clean, current long/short split was not verifiable this run and is left null rather than fabricated.

earnings.note: Q2 beat after Jul 23 close — revenue $16.13B (+25% YoY, fastest in ~15 years), adj EPS $0.42; shares jumped toward $110 after-hours then gave much of it back on profit-taking (stock had dropped ~28% in July into the print). 2026 capex raised to ~$20B. A modest risk-tone positive into the Asia/Europe handover.
Jul 23 post-earnings drop on the lifted 2026 capex guide ($195-205B); still a weight on Nasdaq sentiment.
Jul 23 miss (rev $22.5B, -12% YoY; EPS $0.40); a lingering drag on megacap/risk appetite.

indices.note: Closed -1.90% at $691.96 (range $687.79-$698.66) as $100 oil and rising yields hammered tech; the lowest close of the trailing 21 sessions. Pre-market Jul 24 roughly flat (~$691.22).

dcaSignal.note: Computed from trailing daily closes — relative-cheapness for monthly DCA, not a bottom call.

indices.note: Closed -1.23% at $678.61 (range $675.76-$682.55) on the oil/yields risk-off; after-hours ~$679.15. Sits mid its trailing-monthly range.

dcaSignal.note: Computed from trailing daily closes — relative-cheapness for monthly DCA, not a bottom call.

indices.note: StockAnalysis feed remains STALE — the last print is still dated Jun 18, 2026, not a current session. Per the data-integrity rule, price/changePct are null and dcaSignal is omitted.

calibration.rollingRecord: Range-with-bearish-tilt calls continue to track well; the endday HOLD-above-$64,062 stance held as BTC defended the $65,000 handle overnight and is roughly flat versus the ~$65,047 close.
calibration.note: Yesterday's endday reduce / invalidation at $64,062 did NOT fire — BTC never approached the range floor and prints ~$65,030 this morning, essentially flat. There was nothing to act on and holding the core was correct; pre-emptively reducing would have added cost for no gain (actingHelped=false). The fresh overnight development is the snapped 7-day ETF inflow streak, which tilts the balance slightly more bearish (bear 38 to 39, bull 22 to 21).
